mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 9.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending power, which forces equity valuations downward over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic recovery, boosting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to produce a positive mean 60-day forward return, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income and housing starts indicate strengthening household balance sheets and increased consumer demand, which directly lifts corporate revenues and pushes S&P 500 prices higher.
failure_condition: Positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST coincides with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 215 while the federal funds rate remains near zero, markets anticipate imminent monetary tightening, triggering rapid equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of cases when CPIAUCSL crosses the 215 threshold with low rates.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage bills, which supports consumer spending and corporate profit margins, yielding positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or 60-day returns show no statistical edge.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real GDP and real personal income validate robust aggregate demand, confirming earnings growth trajectories and driving sustained positive S&P 500 performance over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between GDPC1 and RSAFS growth yields a negative or zero mean 20-day forward return across tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks, which depresses corporate earnings forecasts and forces negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes fail to predict negative 5-day S&P 500 returns, with accuracy below 45 percent in out-of-sample tests.
